title: carex vulgaris subsp. reuteriana
intro: Carex vulgaris subsp. reuteriana, also known as the common sedge or wood sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carex vulgaris subsp. reuteriana include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to help reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Carex vulgaris subsp. reuteriana is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
